name: Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop
name_original: Le long de la rivière de l'Adour
description: Cycle the easy 27.1 miles (43.7 km) Along the Adour River route, enjoying scenic river views and historical sites like The Gate of the Basti
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Peyrehorade traverse a varied landscape in the Landes department of Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. The region is characterized by riverine scenery along the Gaves réunis and Adour, rolling hills, and extensive agricultural areas, including distinctive kiwi orchards. While many routes offer low-altitude cycling through the countryside, the proximity to the Pyrenees provides options for more challenging ascents. This diverse topography caters to a range of road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Peyrehorade The most popular road cycling route is Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop, a 27.1 miles (43.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, offering easy cycling along the Adour Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Le Petit Tourmalet – Bastide of Hastingues loop from Peyrehorade, a moderate 45.3 miles (72.9 km) path that explores rolling terrain and historical bastide towns. Local road cyclists also love the Abbaye de Sordes loop from Peyrehorade, a 77.1 miles (124.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the UNESCO World Heritage site of Sorde-l'Abbaye, often completed in about 5 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Peyrehorade is defined by river valleys, rolling agricultural fields, and the Landes countryside, with routes available for various ability levels. The routes in Peyrehorade are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peyrehorade's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Peyrehorade?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Peyrehorade, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes of varying difficulty, from easy rides along riverbanks to more challenging ascents in the rolling hills.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling near Peyrehorade?
A: Road cycling around Peyrehorade offers a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll traverse picturesque riverine scenery along the Gaves réunis and Adour, cycle through rolling agricultural fields, and discover the unique kiwi orchards that characterize the region. Routes also lead through the tranquil Landes countryside, occasionally skirting forests, providing a mix of open views and shaded stretches.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Peyrehorade?
A: Yes, Peyrehorade offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938727545/adour-valley-la-scandiberique-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 43.7 km trail that provides gentle cycling along the Adour Valley.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For more challenging rides, Peyrehorade's proximity to the Pyrenees allows for routes with greater elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24702020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Sordes loop from Peyrehorade&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 124.1 km trail with over 1350 meters of elevation gain, leading through varied landscapes including the UNESCO World Heritage site of Sorde-l'Abbaye. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37756891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Adour River – Berge de l'adour🤩 loop from Peyrehorade&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 240.5 km route with over 1100 meters of climbing.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes allow you to combine cycling with cultural exploration. You can visit the historic bastide town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hastingues&lt;/a&gt;, explore the UNESCO World Heritage site of Sorde-l'Abbaye, or pass by the ruins of the 11th-century Château d'Aspremont. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40988494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Petit Tourmalet – Bastide of Hastingues loop from Peyrehorade&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores rolling terrain and historical bastide towns.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers several accessible routes suitable for families. Many sections along the Gaves réunis and through the agricultural 'Kiwi Valley' are relatively flat and at low altitude, providing pleasant and safe cycling experiences for all ages. Look for routes described as 'easy level' for the most family-friendly options.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Peyrehorade?
A: The road cycling routes around Peyrehorade are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 11 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from tranquil river valleys and agricultural fields to more challenging rolling hills, and the opportunity to discover charming villages and historical sites along the way.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peyrehorade?
A: The region generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, but routes along riverbanks or through shaded sections of the Landes countryside offer relief. Winter cycling is possible, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cyclists?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Peyrehorade are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938727545/adour-valley-la-scandiberique-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29736845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Château de Gramont – La Bastide-Clairence loop from Peyrehorade&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the Château de Gramont.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Peyrehorade?
A: Peyrehorade, as a central hub, offers various parking options within the town, often near the market square or public facilities, from where many routes begin or are easily accessible. Additionally, smaller villages along the routes may have designated parking areas, making it convenient to start your ride from different points.

Q: Can I reach Peyrehorade by public transport with my bike?
A: Peyrehorade is served by regional transport options. While specific rules for bikes on buses or trains can vary, generally, regional trains in France often accommodate bicycles, sometimes with a small fee or during off-peak hours. It's advisable to check the specific train operator's policy for the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region regarding bike transport before your journey.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to see while cycling in the area?
A: Beyond the general riverine and agricultural landscapes, the region is notable for its 'Kiwi Valley,' where cycling routes often pass through distinctive kiwi orchards. The confluence of the Gaves réunis rivers also provides scenic viewpoints and unique natural settings for your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like along the Adour River cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain along the Adour River is quite varied. Many sections are dedicated cycle paths or 'Voie Verte' (greenways) that are paved, smooth, and relatively flat, especially closer to the coast in the 'maritime' part of the Adour. However, if you venture further inland or take specific loops, you might encounter more challenging routes with 'short and dry climbs' or even 'taxing climbs' on dedicated cycle paths, requiring good fitness. Some segments might even necessitate pushing your bike due to steep gradients or unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy a rich variety of landscapes. The route often follows the riverbanks, offering continuous views of the Adour. Keep an eye out for the 'barthes,' which are marshlands home to abundant wildlife like storks, egrets, and grey herons. You'll also pass through cornfields, traditional manor houses, and charming farms. Further inland, the scenery transitions to rolling rural countryside, with historical sites and potential views of the Pyrenees mountains. Don't miss the The Gate of the Bastide of Hastingues, the Scenic route along the Adour and the Gaves, and the dedicated Cycle Path Along the Adour River.

faqs.question: Is the 'Along the Adour River' route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, many sections of the route are very suitable for beginners and all fitness levels, particularly the flat greenways and dedicated cycle paths in the 'maritime' part of the Adour. These sections are often paved and smooth, offering an easy and enjoyable ride. However, be aware that some itineraries, especially those exploring the wider Adour valley or connecting to the Basque Country, can be more challenging with significant climbs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle along the Adour River?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed for much of the year, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but the coastal sections might benefit from sea breezes. Winter can be cooler and potentially wetter, but dedicated paths are still accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route connect to other major cycling networks?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Along the Adour River' route is notably integrated into significant European cycle networks. It forms part of EuroVelo 3 (EV3), also known as 'La Scandibérique,' which connects Scandinavia to the Iberian Peninsula. It also connects to EuroVelo 1 (EV1), 'La Vélodyssée,' which runs along the Atlantic coast. This integration provides extensive options for long-distance touring and varied route planning.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the 'Along the Adour River' route?
faqs.answer: The specific route 'Along the Adour River' described here is approximately 43.7 km (27.1 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 47 minutes to complete on a racebike, according to komoot's estimates. However, the broader Adour River cycling network offers much longer options, such as the 'Sentier de l'Adour,' which is 133 km (82.6 miles) between Dax and Aire-sur-l'Adour, so duration can vary greatly depending on your chosen distance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Adour River cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking locations will depend on your chosen starting point along the extensive Adour River network. Many towns and villages along the river, especially those with dedicated cycle path access, will have public parking available. For detailed information on access points and parking, it's advisable to check local tourism websites or cycling guides for the specific section you plan to ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle along the Adour River?
faqs.answer: No, generally you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle on the public cycle paths and greenways along the Adour River. These routes are freely accessible to the public. However, always respect private property if you venture off the main paths.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in France are dog-friendly, specific rules can vary for dedicated cycle paths and natural reserves like the 'barthes.' It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists and pedestrians. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling environments. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there specific sections recommended for different fitness levels?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. For an easy, flat experience suitable for families or beginners, focus on the 'Voie Verte' (greenway) sections and dedicated cycle paths, particularly in the 'maritime' part of the Adour closer to the coast. For example, the 11 km section between St-Laurent-de-Gosse and Tarnos is well-maintained and flat. If you're an experienced road cyclist seeking a challenge, explore routes further inland or loops near areas like Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren, which are described as 'hard road rides' with significant climbs.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through numerous towns and villages along the Adour River, you'll find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Larger towns will offer more choices, while smaller villages might have a local bakery or a small inn. Planning your stops in advance, especially for longer rides, is recommended to ensure you have access to refreshments and facilities.
